title: Reduce final image size methodically
description: Drive image size down by switching to a slim base, using multi-stage builds, and pruning build-only artifacts rather than guessing.
body: Steps: 1. Inspect the image's layer sizes to find the largest contributors. 2. Choose a minimal base (slim/alpine/distroless) appropriate to the runtime's needs. 3. Use a multi-stage build so compilers, dev dependencies, and caches stay in the build stage and only runtime artifacts ship. 4. Remove package-manager caches and temp files within the same layer that created them. 5. Re-measure and confirm the app still runs. Rollback point: before swapping the base image. Validation: the final image is smaller, layer inspection shows no build toolchain in the runtime stage, and a smoke test confirms the app starts. Risk boundary: do not strip libraries the runtime needs to shave bytes; verify with a runtime smoke test rather than assuming, and escalate if a minimal base breaks required functionality.
